summary: This session discusses the concept of NIL (Name, Image, and Likeness) and its significance for college athletes, particularly following the NCAA's 2021 rule changes that allow athletes to profit from their personal brand. The conversation also explores how collectives can assist athletic programs and businesses in leveraging NIL rights to benefit athletes and enhance marketing strategies.
facts: NIL stands for Name, Image, and Likeness, which allows college athletes to profit from their personal brand. The NCAA adopted new rules in 2021 that permit college athletes to profit from their NIL without losing eligibility. Collectives are groups of athletes that license their NIL rights collectively to negotiate better deals with brands. Collectives can help athletes navigate NIL complexities and protect their eligibility. Businesses can engage athletes through collectives by negotiating sponsorship deals and social media campaigns. Collectives can facilitate endorsement deals and coordinate athlete appearances at events. Collectives can also assist in product collaborations between athletes and businesses.
